# Compatibility Matrix and Benchmark Table

This document summarizes adoption compatibility and baseline benchmark behavior for Week 6.

## Compatibility Matrix

| Surface | Version/Mode | Validation Source | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| Python runtime | `>=3.11` | `pyproject.toml` (`requires-python`) | Supported |
| CI runtime | Python `3.12` | `.github/workflows/ci.yml` and `.github/workflows/release.yml` | Verified in CI |
| Local benchmark runtime | Python `3.14.2` on Windows 11 | `docs/reports/policy-benchmark-summary.json` | Verified locally |
| Embedding model path | `sentence-transformers` available | `ContextWatchdog._get_embedding_model()` path | Supported |
| Contradiction model path | `transformers` zero-shot classifier available | `ContextWatchdog._get_contradiction_classifier()` path | Supported |
| Restricted/offline mode | deterministic fallback embeddings and heuristic conflict scoring | fallback code path in `src/watchdog.py` | Supported |
| Retriever integration | LangChain retriever wrapper | `integrations/langchain_wrapper.py` + tests | Supported |

## Benchmark Method

Command used:

```bash
python scripts/benchmark_policies.py --iterations 300
```

Benchmark characteristics:

1. Deterministic fake embedding and contradiction models (no network/model downloads).
2. Fixed dataset with 12 chunks including duplicates, contradiction, and irrelevant content.
3. Metrics collected: average latency, p50, p95, and kept chunk count.

## Policy Benchmark Table

Measurement artifact: `docs/reports/policy-benchmark-summary.json`
Measured at: `2026-04-28T11:47:49.750703+00:00`

| Policy Recipe | Avg ms | P50 ms | P95 ms | Min ms | Max ms | Avg Kept Chunks | Input Chunks |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| `realtime-support` | 0.522 | 0.457 | 0.864 | 0.400 | 1.482 | 4.0 | 12 |
| `regulated-evidence` | 0.502 | 0.450 | 0.793 | 0.400 | 1.364 | 2.0 | 12 |
| `offline-batch` | 0.632 | 0.499 | 1.271 | 0.400 | 2.048 | 4.0 | 12 |

## Interpretation

1. All three policy contexts run below 1.3 ms at p95 in this deterministic benchmark setup.
2. `regulated-evidence` keeps fewer chunks on average due to stricter relevance and redundancy posture.
3. `offline-batch` allows broader retention while staying within low single-digit millisecond latency.

## Repro Notes

1. Use the same Python runtime and iteration count when comparing future runs.
2. Commit only compact summary artifacts under `docs/reports/`.
3. Re-run benchmarks after policy threshold changes or major scoring algorithm updates.
